changes_made: Renamed k8s_pod_volume_backup_enabled to k8s_persistent_volume_backup_enabled for clarity and consistency
review_notes: The control CP-9-a is applicable to Kubernetes as it involves ensuring backups of critical data, which includes etcd and persistent volumes. The function names mostly follow the conventions, but 'k8s_pod_volume_backup_enabled' was renamed to 'k8s_persistent_volume_backup_enabled' to better reflect the resource being checked. The functions accurately map to Kubernetes features that support backup capabilities.
naming_improvements: Ensure resource names accurately reflect the Kubernetes components being checked, such as using 'persistent_volume' instead of 'pod_volume'.
title: System Backup - Conduct backups of user-level information...
step1_reasoning: The control CP-9-a focuses on conducting backups of user-level information, which can be mapped to Kubernetes by ensuring that etcd, which stores cluster state and secrets, is backed up. Additionally, ensuring that persistent volumes used by pods have backup or snapshot capabilities aligns with the backup requirements of user-level data.
